name: AssemblyAI API
what_it_does: AssemblyAI transcribes audio and video with high accuracy, speaker diarization, sentiment analysis, topic detection, and LeMUR — an LLM layer for asking questions about audio content directly.
use_cases: Transcribing meeting recordings with speaker labels for meeting summary agents
Running LeMUR to ask natural language questions about audio content without separate LLM call
Detecting sensitive information (PII) in audio for compliance and redaction workflows
Sentiment and topic analysis on customer support calls for agent routing
Real-time streaming transcription for voice-enabled agent interfaces
not_for: Ultra-low-latency real-time TTS/STT pipelines (Deepgram or Cartesia for <200ms needs)
Very short clips under a few seconds (API overhead not worth it)
Teams needing on-premises or self-hosted speech processing
best_when: Your agent needs rich audio intelligence beyond raw transcription — speaker identification, topic detection, sentiment, or asking LLM questions about audio without building a separate pipeline.
avoid_when: You only need raw transcription without audio intelligence features — Deepgram or Whisper are cheaper for pure STT.

auth.notes: Single API key in Authorization header. Account-level key with no granular scopes. Separate keys for development vs production recommended via API key rotation.

pricing.paid_tiers: Core transcription: $0.37/hour; Nano model: $0.12/hour; LeMUR: $3/1M LeMUR input tokens
pricing.notes: Audio intelligence features (sentiment, diarization, topics) are addons priced per hour. LeMUR is priced separately per token. Generous free trial credit.

performance.rate_limits: 5 concurrent transcriptions on free tier; contact for higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear status field (queued/processing/completed/error) with error message when applicable. SDK throws typed exceptions. Webhook payloads include status and error fields. Well-documented error codes for common failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each transcript request gets a unique transcript_id. Submitting same audio URL creates a new transcript each time — agents should store transcript_id to avoid duplicate submissions.
known_agent_gotchas: Transcription is async — submit returns transcript_id, agents must poll or use webhook for completion (typically 30-120 seconds)
Audio URL must be publicly accessible at transcription time — pre-signed S3 URLs with short expiry can cause failures if not enough time
LeMUR context window limits — very long transcripts may need chunking before LeMUR can process them
Real-time streaming uses WebSocket, not REST — different SDK methods and connection handling required
Speaker diarization increases processing time significantly — disable if only raw text needed

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope granularity. HIPAA BAA available. SOC 2 Type II. Audio stored temporarily during processing. Audio files deleted after processing unless explicitly stored.
